input.background: A case-control study was conducted to investigate the aetiology of acoustic neuromas. Men aged 25-69 residing in Los Angeles County were eligible for inclusion. A total of 118 men were identified, but 28 were not interviewed due to various reasons. For 86 remaining patients, a neighborhood control of the same race and within five years of age was identified and interviewed. Both cases and controls were interviewed in the same manner to collect information about life experiences, with a focus on exposure to loud noise at work. Overall, 58 cases and 46 controls reported exposure to loud noise at work. Among the case-control pairs, 20 pairs had cases exposed but not controls, and 8 pairs had controls exposed but not cases.
input.question: Calculate the odds ratio for acoustic neuroma associated with exposure to loud noise at work.

output.answer: The odds ratio is estimated as 20 / 8 = 2.5. The 95% CI (method not given in Chapter 10) is 1.05 to 6.56.
